criteria.label: State MCAR, MAR, and MNAR with one concrete example each.
criteria.example: MCAR: random sensor dropout; MAR: men skip optional income more often but among same education missing is random; MNAR: high earners omit income because the value itself is sensitive.

criteria.label: Compute per-column missing percentages and flag columns above a threshold.
criteria.example: On a 4-column DataFrame, write `df.isna().mean()` and list any column with more than 40% missing as a drop or special-category candidate.

criteria.label: Implement train-only median imputation inside a sklearn Pipeline.
criteria.example: Fit `Pipeline([('imputer', SimpleImputer(strategy='median')), ('clf', LogisticRegression())])` on X_train and predict on X_test without refitting the imputer on test.

criteria.label: Add a missingness indicator alongside imputation for one feature.
criteria.example: For column `tenure`, create `tenure_was_missing = X['tenure'].isna().astype(int)` and median-fill `tenure` using statistics from train only.

criteria.label: Explain when to delete, median-impute, or use tree-native NaN for the same column.
criteria.example: For optional `referral_code` at 70% missing: argue drop or "none" category vs median (wrong) vs keeping NaN for LightGBM (reasonable if code structure matters).

criteria.label: Describe how global imputation before splitting causes leakage.
criteria.example: Show that `df['income'].fillna(df['income'].median())` on train+test lets test incomes influence the fill value seen during train preprocessing if done before split.
